data.title: Warren Buffett to donate $6B Berkshire stock to charities
data.description:
Warren Buffett, CEO of Berkshire Hathaway (NYSE:BRK.B) (NYSE:BRK.A), is set to donate nearly $6B worth of his company shares to five charitable organizations on Monday, with the Bill & Melinda Gates Foundation receiving most of the donations.

In a statement late Friday, the Oracle of Omaha said he converted 8,239 Berkshire Hathaway Class A (NYSE:BRK.A) shares into 12,358,500 Class B (NYSE:BRK.B) shares to donate 12,358,321 Class B shares to five foundations.

The Bill & Melinda Gates Foundation Trust, chaired by Microsoft (MSFT) co-founder Bill Gates, will receive more than 9.4M shares. The rest will be divided among four Buffett family foundations, including the Susan Thompson Buffett Foundation, named after the billionaire’s late wife.

The Susan Thompson Buffett Foundation will receive 943,384 shares, while the Sherwood Foundation, Howard G. Buffett Foundation, and NoVo Foundation, all run by Buffett’s three children, will receive 660,366 shares each.

“I owned 474,998 Berkshire A shares worth about $43 billion, and those shares represented more than 98% of my net worth,” Buffett said, adding that he is debt-free and his remaining Class A shares, worth nearly $145 billion, represent 99% of his net worth.

The donations are part of a commitment the 94-year-old made in 2010 under the Giving Pledge, a commitment announced by billionaires including Buffett and Bill Gates to donate the majority of their wealth to address the most pressing social issues.

data.title: Circle Internet, Slide Insurance, IREN, AIFU post biggest weekly swings: financials wrap
data.description:
Financial stocks logged a solid week in which the broader equity market reached a record high on the back of new tariff and geopolitical developments. 

Overall, the Financial Select Sector SPDR ETF (XLF) gained 3.1% for the week ended June 27, compared with the S&P 500's 3.4% climb.

By name, Chinese insurance company AIFU (AIFU) rose the most of any financial stock, surging 36% in the past week;

Iren (IERN), an operator of bitcoin (BTC-USD) mining data centers, jumped 34% as the price of bitcoin (BTC-USD) posted weekly gains and hovered near all-time highs;

CleanSpark (CLSK), which during the week said it achieved its mid-year hashrate target, and crypto exchange Coinbase Global (COIN) gapped up 19% and 15%, respectively; and

Rounding out the five biggest winners, financial services platform SoFi Technologies (SOFI) accelerated 13%. 

On the negative side, Circle Internet Group (CRCL) shed 25% as the stablecoin issuer saw its post-IPO rally stall;

Slide Insurance Holdings (SLDE), which became a publicly-traded property and casualty insurer earlier in the month, retreated 11%;

Brighthouse Financial (BHF) slid 7.5% after news the insurer attracted final takeover bids from two Wall Street firms, both offering a slight premium to the insurer's market value;

Root (ROOT), a provider of auto and renters insurance products, drifted down 7.4%; and

Regional lender Flagstar Financial (FLG) dipped 6.6%.

data.title: Stellantis recalls more than 250K U.S. vehicles over airbag issue
data.description:
• The U.S. National Highway Traffic Safety Administration (NHTSA) announced Saturday that Stellantis (NYSE:STLA) will recall 250,651 vehicles in the U.S. due to an airbag defect found in certain models.
  • The regulator noted that an improperly sealed side curtain airbag has led to the recall, which will affect some of the Pacifica and Voyager vehicles made by the Netherlands-headquartered automaker between 2022 and 2025.
  • The issue relates to airbags sealed improperly during assembly. The NHTSA said Stellantis (NYSE:STLA) dealers will inspect the vehicles, and if anomalies are found, they will replace the side curtain airbags at no cost to customers.
  • The agency added that airbags with inadequate pressure retention can heighten the risk of ejection and injury during an accident. Stellantis (NYSE:STLA) was not immediately available for Seeking Alpha’s requests for comments.

data.title: Notable analyst calls this week: AMD, EA and Uber among top picks
data.description:
The S&P500 (SP500) closed in the green, after the week saw a U.S.-brokered ceasefire between Israel and Iran, a wave of fresh economic data, and a robust outlook from Micron Technology suggesting rising demand for its AI-related chips.

For the week, Nasdaq (COMP:IND) rose 2.7%, while Dow (DJI) advanced 1.9%.

Wall Street had a slew of upgrades and downgrades from analysts. Here are some of the major calls for the week:

Melius upgrades AMD on inferencing potential

Melius Research upgraded AMD (NASDAQ:AMD) to Buy from Hold, citing its potential in inferencing.

Melius analyst Benjamin Reitzes, who also upped his PT to $175 from $110, said that even though shares have gained nearly 25% year-to-date, he has “increased conviction” that investors will gain more confidence in AMD's GPU prospects throughout the year.

“Investors are about to envision even bigger GPU figures in 2026-2028 given a sustainable surge in inferencing, where investing in AMD’s platform seems to be more attractive for hyperscalers and Sovereigns,” noted Reitzes.

CFRA analyst Angelo Zino also raised his rating to Strong Buy from Buy and upped PT to $165 from $125, citing upcoming products and sales.

Uber downgraded on ‘unclear’ future, Lyft bags bullish rating

Canaccord downgraded Uber Technologies (NYSE:UBER) to Hold from Buy, saying that the future is “truly unclear” for the San Francisco-based company.

While Uber was noted to be strong on the ground operations and other tactical elements, analyst Michael Graham warned that the future is unclear as an alternative scenario is that the new world is dominated by a few AV behemoths that control the value chain and leave both Uber and Lyft reflecting on the golden days of the past. 

On the other hand, TD Cowen analyst John Blackledge upgraded Lyft (NASDAQ:LYFT) to Buy from Hold and named the stock his “best smidcap idea for 2025.” 

Blackledge, who also hiked PT for LYFT by 30% to $21, calls Lyft’s customer-focused innovations like Price Lock and partnership with DoorDash “catalysts for growth.”

Roth projects double-digit earnings growth for EA

Roth Capital upgraded Electronic Arts (NASDAQ:EA) to Buy from Neutral, with analyst Eric Handler projecting that the video game giant could see double-digit earnings growth over the next three years.

Handler, who raised PT on EA to $185 from $175, thinks a successful launch of the Battlefield game would be a major near-term catalyst to its stock.

“Sports remains a blue-chip cornerstone for the company’s growth story, but a successful release of a new Battlefield game could materially change investor sentiment,” he said in the research note.

Bank of America, JPMorgan downgraded by Baird

Baird downgraded Bank of America (NYSE:BAC) and JPMorgan Chase (NYSE:JPM), saying that risk/reward trade-off in mega-cap banks is not attractive.

JPM was cut to Underperform from Neutral, with a price target of $235. Baird analysts think that "expectations are super high here" and returns "will likely not be what they've been the last several years at these valuation levels".

Bank of America was lowered to Neutral from Outperform, with a PT of $52.

"Regionals look better to us on a risk/reward basis, as expectations are relatively low compared with the mega caps," added Baird.

Goldman Sachs says Dollar General is fairly priced

Goldman Sachs downgraded Dollar General (NYSE:DG) to Neutral from Buy, warning that an intense competitive environment could impact same-store sales, and pointed to ongoing investment needs in stores and supply chain infrastructure.

The brokerage said while Dollar General was credited for improved fundamentals, the stock's valuation is seen as already pricing that in.

Loop Capital Markets raised PT on Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA) to a new Wall Street high of $250 from $175. Analyst Ananda Baruah cited the belief that hyperscale and AI factor spending could reach $2T by 2028. The brokerage believes Nvidia could hit a $6T market cap on the view that the company essentially has a monopoly in “critical tech” and has strong pricing power.

Wells Fargo maintained its Overweight rating on Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT) but raised the PT to $585 from $565, highlighting the company's 'AI Ambitions' while noting that it is 'still early days.' Analysts led by Michael Turrin said their analysis for Microsoft's AI business suggests over $100B upside case by fiscal year 2029.

UBS gave a triple-digit price target hike to $812 from $683 to Meta Platforms (NASDAQ:META), saying the social media and tech giant stands to benefit from consumer and advertiser demand for AI.

Deutsche Bank upgraded The Estée Lauder Companies (NYSE:EL) to Buy from Hold and set a street-high PT of $95, citing improving fundamentals. The brokerage believes many of the root causes of decline for the company have now been addressed and looking forward, Estée Lauder's ability to be more agile and utilize innovation are seen as advantages.

Jefferies downgraded the Dutch semiconductor equipment companies ASML (NASDAQ:ASML) and ASM International (OTCQX:ASMIY) to Hold from Buy, citing spending concerns.

data.title: OpenAI said to have tapped Google for AI chips in a shift away from Nvidia
data.description:
Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT)-backed artificial intelligence startup OpenAI has started using Google (NASDAQ:GOOG) (NASDAQ:GOOGL)-made AI chips to build its products, including ChatGPT, signaling its first significant move away from an exclusive reliance on Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA) chips, The Information reported.

The deal for GOOG’s tensor processing units (TPU) marks a supplier diversification for OpenAI, which has relied on NVDA chips in the past to train its AI models and for inference computing, a process where model execution takes place after the training is complete.

One of the biggest buyers of Nvidia's (NASDAQ:NVDA) graphics processing units ((GPUs)), the Sam Altman-led company expects Google’s (NASDAQ:GOOG) TPUs rented through Google Cloud will help it drive down costs related to inference, according to the report.

However, the Alphabet (NASDAQ:GOOG) unit is reportedly not renting the most powerful TPUs to OpenAI, signaling its plans to use its most advanced versions to power the company's internal projects, including its own Gemini large language model.

data.title: DoJ settles lawsuit over HPE’s $14B acquisition of Juniper
data.description:
The U.S. Department of Justice has resolved an antitrust lawsuit over Hewlett Packard Enterprise’s (NYSE:HPE) proposed $14B acquisition of its rival Juniper Networks (NYSE:JNPR) less than two weeks before a trial over the case was expected to start.

According to court filings, as part of the settlement, the combined entity will be required to divest HPE’s (NYSE:HPE) Instant On wireless networking business and auction a license related to Juniper's (NYSE:JNPR)  Mist AI software used in its WLAN products.

The deal disclosed late Friday will be subject to court approval and will avoid the need for a trial, which was set to begin on July 9 in San Jose, California.

The settlement will bring an end to a lawsuit the DOJ filed in January to block the acquisition, arguing that it hinders competition, leading to the dominance of only two companies—Cisco Systems (CSCO) and HPE—in the market for networking equipment.

data.title: OpenAI's contribution to Azure AI revenue appears bullish to Morgan Stanley
data.description:
Morgan Stanley finds that OpenAI's revenue contribution to Microsoft Azure over the next few years now appears on track to be more than previously estimated.

"While investors continue to debate the 'Return on Investment' of rising capital expenditures, we see the yields on Microsoft's investments in Generative AI becoming increasingly apparent, both in terms of direct monetization and driving further IT wallet share gains for the broader portfolio," said Morgan Stanley analysts Keith Weiss and Josh Baer, in an extensive investor note.

Morgan Stanley increased its price target to $530 from $482 and reiterated its Overweight rating on Microsoft. Based on new information, Morgan Stanley determined Azure revenue could sustain a year-over-year compound annual growth rate of about 33% through calendar year 2028 compared to its current assumption of 27%. Under this scenario, Azure revenue would break $200B in 2028.

"Contribution from OpenAI is significant and understated in our original framework, which did not include a full 20% revenue share to Microsoft and did not include Fine Tuning or Post Training," Weiss said. "When OpenAI hits the $13 billion investment level, further infrastructure utilization beyond that cap could show up as revenue for the first time (or if OpenAI uses another vendor, Microsoft could monetize those GPU's for other customers). The AI revenue from Microsoft's other customers should also grow rapidly. This supports a mid-30%'s growth rate over the next several years."

Morgan Stanley projects OpenAI's revenue to increase exponentially. This is based on ChatGPT subscription revenue, agents, API revenue and "other revenue," which includes monetization of free users. After totaling $3.7B in 2024, it's expected to leap to $13B in 2025, $29B in 2026, $54B in 2027 and reach $174B by 2030.  

However, longer term, Morgan Stanley noted that OpenAI's existing contract with Microsoft ends in 2030, which could create a revenue "cliff" if the partnership ends.

data.title: Intel’s strategy chief is reportedly leaving the company this month
data.description:
Intel’s (NASDAQ:INTC) top strategy officer, Safroadu Yeboah-Amankwah, is expected to depart the company this month, Reuters reported, citing two people familiar with the matter, adding that the chipmaker confirmed the move.

Yeboah-Amankwah, who joined Intel (NASDAQ:INTC) as chief strategy officer in 2020, is leaving the company on June 30, the people said, requesting anonymity.

His responsibilities included overseeing Intel’s (NASDAQ:INTC) global strategy office, its growth operations, and equity investments.

"We are grateful for Saf’s contributions to Intel and wish him the best," the Santa Clara, California-based tech giant said, confirming the report.

In the wake of Yeboah-Amankwah’s exit, Sachin Katti, who was recently promoted to Intel’s (INTC) chief technology and AI officer, will take over some of his strategy responsibilities.

Meanwhile, the company’s investment arm, Intel Capital, will report to CEO Lip-Bu Tan, according to the people and another source familiar with the matter.

data.title: GE Vernova explores sale of Proficy software unit - Bloomberg
data.description:
GE Vernova (NYSE:GEV) is exploring the sale of its Proficy industrial software business in a deal that could fetch as much as $900M-$1B, Bloomberg reported Friday.

The company is working with financial advisers on the divestiture, and is reaching out to publicly traded companies with industrial software assets as well as to private equity firms, according to the report.

Following its spinoff last year from General Electric, GE Vernova (NYSE:GEV) is benefiting from surging domestic power demand, driven by data centers, new factories and the overall electrification of the economy, which has sparked investor interest in everything to do with power, including electrification software such as Proficy.

GE Vernova's (GEV) Q1 earnings from electrification software, which includes Proficy, rose nearly 9% Y/Y to $224M, according to a company presentation.

data.title: Oil prices reflecting slim chance of supply disruption, fading risk premium, Goldman says
data.description:
The probability of a major oil supply disruption such as a closure of the Strait of Hormuz estimated from options has retreated to just 4% following the Israel-Iran ceasefire, Goldman Sachs analysts said in a note.

Fears that Iran could close the vital waterway after last weekend's U.S. strikes on its nuclear facilities sent Brent crude futures spiking to as high as $81.40/bbl on Monday, but concerns eased suddenly following the truce that was declared the next day, sending crude back below $68/bbl.

"The sharp drop in the geopolitical risk premium likely reflects traders' recent experiences with major geopolitical shocks without significant oil disruptions, Iran's restrained response, strong U.S. and China incentives to avoid large disruptions, and the likely shift to large inventory builds from the fall," Goldman wrote.

The estimated geopolitical risk premium in the spot market has now fallen to below $1/bbl from its Sunday peak of near $15/bbl, the analysts said.

Option markets are forecasting a 60% chance that Brent crude will stay in the $60s in three months and a 28% probability of exceeding $70/bbl, and the market's most likely outcome for December 2025 also is that prices stay in the $60s, according to Goldman, which added that Brent likely would climb to $90/bbl if oil flows through the Strait of Hormuz actually were disrupted.

The week ended with three straight sessions of modest gains in crude oil, but they were nowhere near enough to avoid heavy weekly losses as the Israel-Iran ceasefire and avoidance of supply disruptions took much of the geopolitical risk premium out of the price.

Oil futures are "moving into a consolidation pattern" with support from tighter U.S. inventories, a weaker dollar and the start of the driving season, while greater OPEC+ production is a negative, BOK Financial's Dennis Kissler wrote in a note. "The market seems to be placing value at very near the same levels as before the Iranian conflict started."

Oil prices closed slightly higher on Friday, recovering from a midday drop following a report that OPEC+ was weighing another super-sized oil output boost in August.

Eight key OPEC+ members have agreed on 411K bbl/day increases in each of the previous three months, and several delegates reportedly are ready to consider the same hike again for August when the cartel convenes on July 6.

This week, front-month Nymex crude (CL1:COM) for August delivery closed -11.3% to $65.52/bbl and front-month Brent crude (CO1:COM) for August ended -12% to $67.77/bbl, in the largest one-week net and percentage decline for both benchmarks since March 17, 2023.

Also this week, front-month Nymex RBOB gasoline (XB1:COM) for July delivery settled -10.3% to $2.0898/gal and front-month July heating oil (HO1:COM) finished -9.2% to $2.3072/gal.

On Friday, Nymex crude rose 0.4%, Brent added less than 0.1%, Nymex gasoline fell 0.4%, and Nymex heating oil lost 2.2%.

U.S. natural gas futures (NG1:COM) rebounded Friday after five straight sessions of losses, as the August front-month contract gained 6% but ended -5.3% on the week to $3.739/MMBtu.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:USO), (BNO), (UCO), (SCO), (USL), (DBO), (DRIP), (GUSH), (USOI), (UNG), (BOIL), (KOLD), (UNL), (FCG)

Energy stocks, as represented by the Energy Select Sector SPDR Fund (NYSEARCA:XLE), were the worst performing group this week, -4.1%.

Top 10 gainers in energy and natural resources in the past 5 days: Ascent Solar Technologies (ASTI) +106.4%, Geospace Technologies (GEOS) +38.3%, Korea Electric Power (KEP) +32.1%, Eco Wave Power (WAVE) +28.2%, Critical Metals (CRML) +25.3%, Fuel Tech (FTEK) +23.8%, Ivanhoe Electric (IE) +22.7%, Enovix (ENVX) +20.2%, Solaredge Technologies (SEDG) +19.8%, Sunrun (RUN) +19.2%.

Top 10 decliners in energy and natural resources in the past 5 days: Robin Energy (RBNE) -45.2%, Indonesia Energy (INDO) -38.3%, Stem (STEM) -23.1%, Vital Energy (VTLE) -20.6%, VivoPower International (VVPR) -20.3%, Opal Fuels (OPAL) -20%, Mexco Energy (MXC) -20%, Prairie Operating (PROP) -18.9%, Houston American Energy (HUSA) -18.8%, Marine Petroleum Trust (MARPS) -17.6%.

Source: Barchart.com
